title: Crispy Tunisian Steak Tagine
description: A fresh, flaky onepot Tunisian dish featuring skirt steak caramelized to perfection in a fragrant sauce of spices, harissa, and herbs.
instructions: Trim the skirt steak and pat dry with paper towels.
In a shallow dish, combine steak with smoked paprika, ground cumin, salt, and pepper. Let it rest for 5 min.
Heat olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed tagine or Dutch oven over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Add the steak pieces and sear until the edges turn golden brown, about 3 min per side. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook in batches if necessary.
Remove steak and set aside. In the same pan, add sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 min.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for 30 s until fragrant.
Add the harissa paste and cook, stirring, for 1 min to release its flavors.
Return the steak to the pan, toss to coat with the sauce, and let everything simmer gently for 5 min to meld the flavors.
Finish with chopped coriander, sprinkle additional salt if needed, and serve hot, optionally with lemon wedges or a side of couscous.
